notes.introduction: A verse-by-verse study of Genesis 32:1-23 tracing Jacob's actions and emotions as he prepares to reunite with his brother Esau after a twenty-year separation, covering the messengers he sends, his prayer, and the present he assembles for Esau.
outline: I. The Preparation of Jacob (vv. 1-8): Jacob sends messengers to Esau with a specific message and divides his company upon hearing Esau is approaching with 400 men
II. The Prayer of Jacob (vv. 9-12): Jacob prays, acknowledging his unworthiness, expressing fear, and appealing to the promises and direction God had given him
III. The Present for Esau (vv. 13-23): Jacob assembles a large gift of livestock, sends it ahead in successive droves to appease Esau, and moves his family across the ford Jabbok
